target: data/sections.json part6 — SEC-6.3 The Schumann Math Error (full rewrite)
replacement_html:
6.3 The Schumann Height Inconsistency

WIN-029 claims the dome’s cavity height matches the Schumann-derived height to “5.3% error.” The arithmetic is correct — but only because the dome compares to a firmament height (9,086 km) that its own H(r) curve cannot produce. The dome’s three reference heights (9,572 km, 9,086 km, and 8,537 km) are mutually inconsistent under its own equations.

WIN-029 (Schumann resonance): Claims the dome’s cavity height matches the Schumann-derived height to “5.3% error.” The derivation: HSchumann = c / (4 × 7.83 Hz) = 9,572 km. The dome’s evolution page explicitly states this is compared to its “model parameterization” of 9,086 km: (9,572 − 9,086) / 9,086 = 5.35%, rounding to 5.3%. The arithmetic is correct.

The problem is the number it compares to. The dome’s own height function H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) has a strict global maximum of 8,537 km at r = 0 (the pole) and decreases monotonically outward. The claimed “model parameterization” of 9,086 km exceeds this maximum. The dome describes 9,086 km as a “near-pole average,” but because H(r) never reaches 9,086 km at any radius, no averaging scheme can produce it — the elementary bound E[f(X)] ≤ sup f(X) applies. Three firmament heights now coexist without reconciliation: HSchumann = 9,572 km (back-calculated from 7.83 Hz), the model parameter = 9,086 km (exceeds H(r) maximum), and H(r=0) = 8,537 km (the curve’s actual peak). None of these pairs agree to better than 5.3%, and the largest gap — between the Schumann inversion and the real H(r) peak — is (9,572 − 8,537) / 8,537 = 12.1%. The V12 “reconciliation” of these heights (claiming they sample the curve at different radii) was disproven the moment the exponential was specified: 9,086 km and 9,572 km lie outside the curve’s range. (See Section 2.1 for the full impossibility proof.)

The frequency problem is even worse. The quarter-wave resonance fQW(r) = c / (4 × H(r)) is lowest at the pole: fQW(0) = 300,000 / (4 × 8,537) ≈ 8.77 Hz. Since H(r) only decreases from there, fQW(r) only increases — every point on the dome produces a frequency above 8.77 Hz, and no spatial average can fall below it. The observed 7.83 Hz is unreachable by the dome’s own geometry at any location. The author obtains 7.83 Hz by silently switching to the globe’s spherical cavity formula f ≈ c / (2πR), where R = 6,371 km is the globe Earth’s radius — a formula that requires the spherical geometry the dome rejects.

Kernel of truth: Back-calculating cavity geometry from resonance frequencies is legitimate physics — Schumann (1952) and Balser & Wagner (1960) did exactly this for the Earth-ionosphere cavity. The dome correctly identifies the technique. But applying it with the wrong resonance formula (quarter-wave linear waveguide instead of spherical eigenmode) and comparing the result to a height the model’s own curve cannot produce does not constitute a successful prediction.
holes_addressed: MAJOR: Corrected misidentification of comparison target — dome compares to 9,086 km (model parameterization), not 8,537 km (H(r=0)). Our prior claim that the 5.3% was arithmetically wrong was itself wrong.
MODERATE: Changed 'parallel-plate cavity geometry' framing to describe the actual exponential cavity and quarter-wave frequency analysis, consistent with Section 2.1.
MODERATE: Section is now self-contained with the key frequency impossibility argument (8.77 Hz minimum) inline, not just cross-referenced.
new_evidence_added: Dome evolution page quote: 'Matches model H = 9,086 km to 5.3%' confirming 9,086 km is the comparison target
Dome model page quote: 'Model parameterization (9,086 km) = near-pole average' confirming the claimed derivation
Triple inconsistency framing: 9,572 vs 9,086 vs 8,537 km
Inline frequency impossibility: f_QW(0) = 8.77 Hz > 7.83 Hz, unreachable at any radius

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome defender says: Your old Section 6.3 was wrong about the comparison target. How can we trust the replacement?
anticipated_objections.response: The rewrite leads with the correction, explicitly acknowledging the 5.3% arithmetic is correct. This demonstrates intellectual honesty and makes the subsequent impossibility argument more credible, not less. We caught our own error — the dome has not caught its 9,086 km impossibility.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome claims 9,086 km is a 'near-pole average' of H(r), not the peak value.
anticipated_objections.response: H(r) = 8537*exp(-r/8619) has sup = 8,537 km. By E[f(X)] ≤ sup f(X), no averaging of H(r) can exceed 8,537 km. The 'near-pole average' claim is mathematically impossible regardless of the weighting scheme. This is proved rigorously in Section 2.1.

anticipated_objections.objection: Maybe the dome will update H(r) to reach 9,086 km (e.g. different functional form).
anticipated_objections.response: Any H(r) with H(0) = 8,537 and the stated exponential form is bounded by 8,537. Changing the functional form to reach 9,086 would break the λ_g = 8,619 scale length that the dome claims appears across six phenomena. The fix creates a cascade of inconsistencies in other WINs.

anticipated_objections.objection: The title change from 'Math Error' to 'Height Inconsistency' weakens the framing.
anticipated_objections.response: It strengthens it. 'Math Error' was inaccurate (the 5.3% arithmetic is correct). 'Height Inconsistency' describes the actual, deeper problem: three mutually incompatible heights. The new title is both accurate and more damaging.

kernel_of_truth: Back-calculating cavity dimensions from resonance frequencies is legitimate physics (Schumann 1952, Balser & Wagner 1960). The dome correctly identifies this technique and applies it to the Schumann fundamental. The 5.3% arithmetic between 9,572 km and 9,086 km is genuinely correct. The failure is not in the technique or the arithmetic, but in comparing the result to a reference value (9,086 km) that the dome's own exponential height function cannot produce at any radius or under any averaging scheme.

section_title_change.reason: The old title accused the dome of an arithmetic error that does not exist. The new title accurately describes the real problem: three mutually incompatible firmament heights.

credibility_risk_resolved: HIGH. The prior Section 6.3 contained a factual error that a dome defender could exploit to discredit the entire review. The curmudgeon rated the defense at 5/5 survivability. This rewrite eliminates the vulnerability while replacing it with a strictly stronger argument.
